comment: the real hook via guard_check (see reference_guard_check_tester memory).
Every case uses `ls` / `git status` as the trusted inner verb, so the
expected verdicts test the guard's STRUCTURE, not this project's allow
list. Removing `ls` from permissions.allow would invalidate the corpus.
decision: allow = auto-approved, deny = hard floor, ask = guard actively
prompts with a reason, silent = guard abstains so the native permission
engine decides (silent is NOT the same as 'will not prompt').
MACHINE-DEPENDENT: the rm cases hard-code the scratchpad prefix, which
the hook derives as /tmp/claude-<uid>/<repo-root-with-dashes>/. On a
different uid or checkout path those three cases go red for an
environmental reason, not a real regression - update the paths.
